item.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the No. 2 parking brake cable assembly and No. 3 parking brake cable assembly.
The procedures listed below are for the No. 3 parking brake cable assembly.

s-1.ptxt: PRECAUTION
DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL (for Manual Seat)

atten2.ptxt: Wait for 90 seconds after disconnecting the cable to prevent the airbag working. (See page )

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT SEAT ASSEMBLY LH (for Manual Seat)

atten4.ptxt: Refer to the procedures up to "REMOVE FRONT SEAT ASSEMBLY (for Manual Seat)". (See page )

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT SEAT ASSEMBLY RH (for Manual Seat)

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT SEAT ASSEMBLY LH (for Power Seat)

atten4.ptxt: Refer to the procedures up to "REMOVE FRONT SEAT ASSEMBLY (for Power Seat)". (See page )

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT SEAT ASSEMBLY RH (for Power Seat)

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE PARKING BRAKE LEVER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Manual Transaxle)

atten4.ptxt: Refer to the procedures up to "REMOVE PARKING BRAKE LEVER". (See page )

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 PARKING BRAKE CABLE ASSEMBLY (for Manual Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, pull up the parking brake lever claw.
Remove the lock nut, the No. 1 wire adjusting nut, and the No. 1 parking brake cable assembly from the parking brake lever sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE PARKING BRAKE PEDAL ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

atten4.ptxt: Refer to the procedures up to "REMOVE PARKING BRAKE PEDAL". (See page )

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 PARKING BRAKE CABLE ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, pull up the parking brake pedal claw.
Remove the lock nut and the No. 1 wire adjusting nut.
Using needle nose pliers, remove the clip and the No. 1 parking brake cable from the parking brake pedal assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE LUGGAGE DOOR LOCK OPEN LEVER SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Remove the screw.
Disengage the claw and remove the luggage door open lever sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT FLOOR CARPET ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Disengage the 6 clamps and the 2 claws.
Turn back the front floor carpet assembly until the bolts of the cross member floor No. 4 reinforcement sub-assembly can be seen.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE CROSS MEMBER FLOOR NO. 4 REINFORCEMENT SUB-ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 8 bolts and the cross member floor No. 4 reinforcement sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: SEPARATE NO. 3 PARKING BRAKE CABLE ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Separate the No. 3 parking brake cable assembly from the parking brake equalizer.

s-1.ptxt: SEPARATE NO. 2 PARKING BRAKE CABLE ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Separate the No. 2 parking brake cable assembly from the parking brake equalizer.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 4 PARKING BRAKE CABLE ASSEMBLY (for Automatic Transaxle)

s2.ptxt: Slide the rubber boot as shown in the illustration.
Remove the No. 4 parking brake cable assembly from the parking brake equalizer as shown in the illustration.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE CENTER EXHAUST PIPE ASSEMBLY (for 2AZ-FE)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts, 2 nuts and center exhaust pipe assembly.
Remove the gasket from the front exhaust pipe.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE EXHAUST TAIL PIPE ASSEMBLY (for 2GR-FE)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 4 nuts and 2 exhaust tail pipes.
Remove the 2 gaskets from the exhaust center pipe assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE EXHAUST CENTER PIPE ASSEMBLY (for 2GR-FE)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts, 2 nuts and exhaust center pipe assembly.
Remove the gasket from the exhaust pipe assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 2 FRONT FLOOR HEAT LOWER INSULATOR (for 2AZ-FE)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 3 nuts and the No. 2 front floor heat lower insulator.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR WHEEL
SEPARATE REAR DISC BRAKE CALIPER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and separate the rear disc brake caliper assembly.

atten4.ptxt: Do not disconnect the flexible hose from the disc brake caliper assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR DISC

s2.ptxt: Release the parking brake and place the matchmarks on the rear disc and the axle hub.
Remove the rear disc.

atten4.ptxt: If the disc cannot be removed easily, turn the shoe adjuster until the wheel turns freely.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE PARKING BRAKE SHOE RETURN TENSION NO. 1 SPRING

s2.ptxt: Using needle-nose pliers, remove the 2 parking brake shoe return tension No. 1 springs.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE PARKING BRAKE SHOE STRUT

s2.ptxt: Remove the parking brake shoe strut and the parking brake shoe strut compression spring.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 PARKING BRAKE SHOE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Release the claw of the parking brake shoe hold down spring No. 1 cup.
Remove the No. 1 parking brake shoe assembly as shown in the illustration.

Remove the parking brake shoe hold down spring No. 1 cup, the parking brake shoe hold down spring, the parking brake shoe hold down spring No. 2 cup, and the parking brake shoe hold down spring No. 1 pin.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE PARKING BRAKE SHOE ADJUSTING SCREW SET

s2.ptxt: Remove the parking brake shoe adjusting screw set.
Remove the parking brake shoe return tension No. 2 spring.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 2 PARKING BRAKE SHOE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Release the claw of the parking brake shoe hold down spring No. 1 cup.
Remove the No. 2 parking brake shoe assembly as shown in the illustration.

Remove the parking brake shoe hold down spring No. 1 cup, the parking brake shoe hold down spring, the parking brake shoe hold down spring No. 2 cup, and the parking brake shoe hold down spring No. 2 pin.
Using needle-nose pliers, disconnect the No. 3 parking brake cable assembly from the parking brake shoe lever.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the No. 3 parking brake cable assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 3 PARKING BRAKE CABLE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 bolts and separate the No. 3 parking brake cable assembly from the backing plate.
Using needle-nose pliers, remove the clip from the No. 3 parking brake cable assembly.
Remove the 3 bolts (A), the bolt (B), and the nut (C), and disengage each claw.
Remove the No. 3 parking brake cable assembly from the body.
Remove the parking brake cable clamp.
